channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump, Biden react to death of former President Jimmy Carter
item.description: President-elect Trump and President Biden both reacted to the death of former President Jimmy Carter, who died at his home in Georgia on Sunday at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Camp David: Jimmy Carter's finest moment
item.description: President Jimmy Carter's crowning foreign policy achievement, the Camp David Accords of 1978, was the first peace agreement brokered in the Middle East since Israel became a nation in 1948.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kansas once required voters to prove citizenship. That didn't work out so well
item.description: Republicans made claims about illegal voting by noncitizens a centerpiece of their 2024 campaign messaging.
item.dc:creator: John Hanna, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In shift, Trump downgrades soaring rhetoric on campaign promises
item.description: President-elect Trump on the campaign trail made grandiose promises to voters to bring down costs quickly, to end the war in Ukraine before he even took office and to use tariffs to bolster the U.S. economy and manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: European officials pitch new idea to shore up defenses with Trump's return
item.description: European officials are struggling to agree on a plan to shore up hundreds of billions of dollars to bolster defenses. Some NATO experts are proposing a global defense bank.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why Trump is targeting Panama, Greenland, Canada
item.description: President-elect Trump has spent parts of the holiday season vowing to take over the Panama Canal, calling for the U.S. to buy Greenland from Denmark and suggesting Canada could become the 51st state. It all suggests Trump is focused on somehow expanding the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats grapple with 'jarring' lack of diversity in DNC race
item.description: The race for Democratic National Committee (DNC) chair is shaping up to be a field of largely white men — a notable development for a party that has long touted diversity within its ranks. Roughly half a dozen candidates are running to helm the Democratic Party, almost all of them men.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to delay TikTok ban so he can weigh in after he takes office
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump asked the Supreme Court on Friday to pause the potential TikTok ban from going into effect until his administration can pursue a “political resolution” to the issue.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge signals that contempt hearing for Rudy Giuliani over his assets might not go well for him
item.description: A federal judge is signaling that Rudy Giuliani's contempt hearing next Friday might not end so well for the former New York City mayor and onetime personal lawyer for President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Janet Yellen tells Congress US could hit debt limit in mid-January
item.description: Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen says her agency will need to start taking “extraordinary measures,” or special accounting maneuvers intended to prevent the nation from hitting the debt ceiling, as early as January 14th, in a letter sent to congressional leaders Friday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Republicans gain edge in Minnesota House after DFLer drops out over residency case
item.description: Republicans gain one-seat majority heading into 2025 session as a DFL-won seat becomes vacant. A special election will come early next year.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: An online debate over foreign workers in tech shows tensions in Trump's political coalition
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — An online spat between factions of Donald Trump's supporters over immigration and the tech industry has thrown internal divisions in his political movement into public display, previewing the fissures and contradictory views his coalition could bring to the White House.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US to send $1.25 billion in weapons to Ukraine, pushing to get aid out before Biden leaves office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States is expected to announce that it will send $1.25 billion in military assistance to Ukraine, U.S. officials said Friday, as the Biden administration pushes to get as much aid to Kyiv as possible before leaving office on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US sanctions the founder of Georgia's ruling political party
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration has imposed sanctions on the founder of Georgia’s ruling political party, which has steered the country away from a pro-Western stance and towards Russia, U.S. officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Manchin objects to Biden's commutations
item.description: Outgoing Sen. Joe Manchin (I-W.Va.) is denouncing as “misguided” and “disgusting” President Biden's move to commute the sentences of two men convicted of murdering a student at Marshall University.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers push to expand agencies' power to cope with drones
item.description: Lawmakers continue to discuss legislation that would expand the authority of local police agencies to deal with drones.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: President Biden signs 50 bills into law
item.description: Before leaving to spend the rest of the holiday in the Virgin Islands, President Joe Biden signed 50 bills into law on Christmas Eve, as his time in office comes to an end.
item.dc:creator: Traci Caldwell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bald eagle finally becomes official US bird
item.description: The eagle had long been associated with the U.S., but it only just officially became the national bird.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Americans are exhausted by political news. TV ratings and a new AP-NORC poll show they're tuning out
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As a Democrat who immersed himself in political news during the presidential campaign, Ziad Aunallah has much in common with many Americans since the election. He's tuned out. “People are mentally exhausted,” said Aunallah, 45, of San Diego.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Opinion: Elon Musk wants to ‘delete’ many Americans' financial lifeline
item.description: It is especially surprising that one of the first federal agencies to come under scrutiny from the incoming administration is one that has returned billions of dollars to many of the same consumers who were counting on leaders in Washington to look out for their wallets.
item.dc:creator: Sharon McGowan, opinion contributor, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bill Clinton discharged after hospitalization for flu
item.description: Former President Clinton was discharged Tuesday morning after being hospitalized a day earlier for flu, according to his spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Former lawmaker Mary Murphy, longest-serving woman in the House, suffers stroke
item.description: Former Rep. Mary Murphy experienced complications after a stroke last week and will likely be transferred to hospice care, House Speaker Melissa Hortman said.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Biden didn't remove these 3 inmates from death row
item.description: Those who received the Monday commutations of their sentences saw them go from execution to life with no possibility of parole. However, three “hard cases” were not among the commutations.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Broken tie in Minnesota House would give GOP muscle at start of 2025 session
item.description: Even with a slight majority, Republicans could flex newfound power at the start of the legislative session if DFL lawmaker’s disqualification stands.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News
